input.background: Assume \(\hat{\theta}_1\) and \(\hat{\theta}_2\) are the two estimators of parameter \(\theta\). Suppose that \(E(\hat{\theta}_1)=0.9\theta\), \(E(\hat{\theta}_2)=\theta\), \(\operatorname{var}(\hat{\theta}_1)=2\), and \(\operatorname{var}(\hat{\theta}_2)=3\).
input.question: Which estimator would you prefer based on the given information?

output.answer:
We have
$$
\begin{gathered}
M S E \text{ of } \theta_1=\operatorname{var}(\hat{\theta})+(\text{ bias })^2=2+0.01 \theta^2 . \\
M S E \text{ of } \theta_2=\operatorname{var}(\hat{\theta})+(\text{ bias })^2=3+0=3 .
\end{gathered}
$$

Thus, if \(|\theta|<10\) then \(\hat{\theta}_1\) would be preferred. If \(|\theta|>10\) then \(\hat{\theta}_2\) is preferred.
